# Bhagavad Gita Image Prompts for GPT Image 2

Each prompt is standalone. Paste one prompt at a time into GPT Image 2.
Global direction used throughout: strong warriors, mature age cues, Krishna as the strongest and visually largest divine presence, no text inside images.

## Chapter 1 - Arjuna Visada Yoga

### 1.1

Bhagavad Gita 1.1 image prompt. Create a cinematic, reverent Mahabharata-era Indian epic image for GPT Image 2. Use rich natural color, detailed ancient armor, silk, gold ornaments, carved chariots, dust, flags, and a vast Kurukshetra battlefield atmosphere. All warriors shown must look physically strong, disciplined, and battle-ready; no weak or comic bodies. Lord Krishna must be the strongest and visually largest presence in the composition: mature divine adult, blue skin, broad powerful build, calm commanding face, radiant golden aura, peacock-feather crown, yellow silk, unmistakably supreme even when seated as charioteer. Arjuna should be a mature powerful warrior archer with Gandiva, noble face, athletic build, not a teenager. If Bhishma or Drona appear, show them as elderly but extremely formidable warrior elders, tall, muscular, white-haired or grey-haired, with authority. Avoid modern objects, gore, severed bodies, caricature, text, captions, watermarks, logos, or extra fingers. Square 1:1 composition, centered epic framing, ultra-detailed, devotional but realistic, dramatic cinematic lighting. Chapter context: Opening battlefield context: two immense armies face each other at Kurukshetra, conches, banners, elephants, horses, and chariots under a tense dawn sky. Verse meaning to visualize: Dhritarashtra said: Sanjaya, my sons and the sons of Pandu have gathered at Kurukshetra, the field of dharma, ready for war. What did they do? Tone: tense, sorrowful, morally heavy, pre-battle realism. Include character cues: blind elder king Dhritarashtra in a royal hall, strong but aged, listening intensely; Sanjaya as a mature seer-narrator with composed face and visionary gaze. Visual direction: Emphasize army inspection, conches, commanders, banners, and the rising tension before battle; show both sides as powerful and disciplined. Make Krishna visibly larger, stronger, and more radiant than every warrior, yet serene and compassionate. Make Arjuna and all warriors powerful and age-appropriate; show no children unless the verse context specifically requires a younger warrior like Abhimanyu. Do not render any written Sanskrit or English words inside the image.

Include character cues: Bhishma as an elderly but gigantic and mighty white-haired warrior elder; Bhima as a massive muscular warrior with mace-like strength. Use rich natural color, detailed ancient armor, silk, gold ornaments, carved chariots, dust, flags, and a vast Kurukshetra battlefield atmosphere. All warriors shown must look physically strong, disciplined, and battle-ready; no weak or comic bodies. Arjuna should be a mature powerful warrior archer with Gandiva, noble face, athletic build, not a teenager. Chapter context: Krishna begins the teaching: Arjuna sits in crisis on the chariot while Krishna radiates calm strength and reveals wisdom about atma, duty, and steady mind. Verse meaning to visualize: You have a claim only over karma, never over its results. Do not make the result of karma your motive, and do not become attached to akarma, or doing nothing. Tone: wise, heroic, contemplative, luminous. Keep Krishna and Arjuna central on the chariot unless the verse specifically calls for Dhritarashtra, Sanjaya, or another warrior. Visual direction: Visualize karma as purposeful action: Arjuna releases effort toward duty while distant fruits dissolve, showing action without attachment to results. Make Krishna visibly larger, stronger, and more radiant than every warrior, yet serene and compassionate. Make Arjuna and all warriors powerful and age-appropriate; show no children unless the verse context specifically requires a younger warrior like Abhimanyu. Do not render any written Sanskrit or English words inside the image.

Verse meaning to visualize: Arjuna said: If You think that knowledge is superior to karma, O Krishna, why then, O Keshava, do You ask me to engage in this terrible karma? Tone: wise, heroic, contemplative, luminous. Include character cues: Arjuna as a mature powerful archer holding Gandiva; Krishna as the supreme divine charioteer, largest and strongest presence. Make Krishna visibly larger, stronger, and more radiant than every warrior, yet serene and compassionate. Make Arjuna and all warriors powerful and age-appropriate; show no children unless the verse context specifically requires a younger warrior like Abhimanyu. ### 6.1

Use rich natural color, detailed ancient armor, silk, gold ornaments, carved chariots, dust, flags, and a vast Kurukshetra battlefield atmosphere. All warriors shown must look physically strong, disciplined, and battle-ready; no weak or comic bodies. Lord Krishna must be the strongest and visually largest presence in the composition: mature divine adult, blue skin, broad powerful build, calm commanding face, radiant golden aura, peacock-feather crown, yellow silk, unmistakably supreme even when seated as charioteer. Arjuna should be a mature powerful warrior archer with Gandiva, noble face, athletic build, not a teenager. If Bhishma or Drona appear, show them as elderly but extremely formidable warrior elders, tall, muscular, white-haired or grey-haired, with authority. Avoid modern objects, gore, severed bodies, caricature, text, captions, watermarks, logos, or extra fingers. Verse meaning to visualize: Whoever offers Me with devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit or a little water that, so offered devotedly by the pure-minded, I accept. Tone: devotional, intimate, protective, luminous. Keep Krishna and Arjuna central on the chariot unless the verse specifically calls for Dhritarashtra, Sanjaya, or another warrior. Visual direction: Show the restless mind as storm winds around the chariot becoming calm under Krishna's steady gaze and Arjuna's discipline. Make Krishna visibly larger, stronger, and more radiant than every warrior, yet serene and compassionate. Make Arjuna and all warriors powerful and age-appropriate; show no children unless the verse context specifically requires a younger warrior like Abhimanyu. Do not render any written Sanskrit or English words inside the image.

Tone: wise, heroic, contemplative, luminous. Keep Krishna and Arjuna central on the chariot unless the verse specifically calls for Dhritarashtra, Sanjaya, or another warrior. Visual direction: Show Krishna's vibhutis radiating outward: sun, moon, mountains, river, lion, eagle, thunderbolt, sages, and heroic power arranged around him. Make Krishna visibly larger, stronger, and more radiant than every warrior, yet serene and compassionate. Make Arjuna and all warriors powerful and age-appropriate; show no children unless the verse context specifically requires a younger warrior like Abhimanyu. Do not render any written Sanskrit or English words inside the image.

## Chapter 15 - Purushottama Yoga

### 15.1

## Chapter 17 - Sraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga

### 17.1

Bhagavad Gita 17.1 image prompt. Create a cinematic, reverent Mahabharata-era Indian epic image for GPT Image 2. Chapter context: Faith context: Krishna teaches three kinds of faith, food, sacrifice, self-discipline, gifts, and Om Tat Sat through symbolic ritual scenes. Verse meaning to visualize: Arjuna said: Those who, setting aside the ordinances of the scriptures, perform sacrifice with faith, what is their condition, O Krishna? Is is clarity, passion or darkness? Tone: ethical, serious, contrast-rich. Make Krishna visibly larger, stronger, and more radiant than every warrior, yet serene and compassionate. Make Arjuna and all warriors powerful and age-appropriate; show no children unless the verse context specifically requires a younger warrior like Abhimanyu.
